examples: Tis not much, your Majesty, yet perchance it may beguile a short half-hour for want of a better.
The young pale wind-flowers had arisen by the wood, and under the hazels, when perchance the hot sun pushed his way, new little suns dawned and blazed with the real light.
Perchance not deep, but intense and rapid.
And what, perchance, can we expect in return for all that money?
There had perchance been some pre-weeping, by him, in the woods.
